best.explanation: Belgas looks like a clean fun-sized north coast window for Rare window, Medium confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 144 kJ, 11 s period, workable consistency 60/100, and good wind at 2 km/h.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: tide not captured, confidence reduced.

A dependable left-hand reef that turns modest swell into real rides; this is best for clean, playful walls when the bigger beaches are too wild or too small. ranked.explanation: Almagreira looks like a clean fun-sized north coast window for Rare window, Medium confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 82 kJ, 13 s period, workable consistency 59/100, and glassy conditions.  An exposed north-coast beach break with more raw swell than Baleal; this is best for small, clean north-coast days when protected spots look underpowered; 10-13s at mid tide is the Goldilocks zone for exposed beaches, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Expect: banks, rips and wind exposure decide whether it is magic or messy.

ranked.explanation: Meio da Baia looks like a clean fun-sized window for Worth checking, Medium confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 101 kJ, 11 s period, workable consistency 60/100, and glassy conditions.  An inconsistent Baía spot that can work well when north-northwest swell reaches the bay; this is best for bodyboard sessions when the bay is clean, there is enough period and size, and the forecast shows a defined wave; 10-13s at mid tide is the Goldilocks zone for exposed beaches.  Expect: it is less reliable than the main bay spots, so weak swell or short period can make it feel soft.
ranked.shortExplanation: Meio da Baia is an inconsistent Baía spot that can work well when north-northwest swell reaches the bay · label Worth checking, Medium confidence · 0.6-0.9 m · 101 kJ · 11 s period · 10-13s at mid tide is the Goldilocks zone for exposed beaches · glassy conditions.
